tokens: I'm over here (at) the back bay.
I'm ready to start the ALSEP off-loading for 'em.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got something for you and I'm coming around.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Say, I've noticed when you get started moving down here, well, it's sometimes hard to stop.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hold on to this (probably Pete's saddlebag) a second...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete and Al, you're 1 plus 22 into the timeline, and you're running about 6 minutes behind nominal.
We're monitoring PLSS feedwater 2 as a determining parameter.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We've kind of gotten over the initial checkout on how to walk and move around; and maybe we won't have any problem with this hardware like we did with the TV...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And we're showing 2 plus 30 left in the EVA.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We ought to be able to move out with this thing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The experiment bay looks real good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The LM exterior looks beautiful the whole way around.
Not a lot that doesn't look the way it did the day we launched it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Possibly pulling a lanyard to open the SEQ bay doors) Light one.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Now we) just lay it on the lunar surface.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Here it (probably the first package) comes.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Riding right out on the boom, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Wanting to take a picture) Look at me, Pete.
The LM and everything's reflecting in your visor.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Long Pause) Right out on the boom, just like advertised.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait until I get this (meaning the first ALSEP package) out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Going to look like the back of the flight crew training building in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: SEQ Bay door is coming closed.
Push that door...(Pause) What am I hung up on.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get it off for you.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, lift your left foot up and you're okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: High Gain Antenna: Pitch, minus 13; Yaw, 225.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Hey, Houston, do you hear this constant beeping in the background?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We've heard it now for about the past 45 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Responding to the second half of Gibson's communication) That's right, so have we.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Static returns briefly) Intrepid, we've tried to isolate it.
It appears it's something on the downlink coming from the LM.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And there's the lunar tools all set up for you, fella.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause; static has cleared) Old Chuck Weatherred will be happy to know we're throwing it up for him, here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, what do you need of these?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How about the...Here, let me put that on.
You put that in the package 2, and I'll pick that up later.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Excuse me, Pete, I'll move it (possibly the Hand Tool Carrier) over and plant it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got to put this together right.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Shall I let down the cask while we wait?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, we're going to go ahead and pull down the fuel cask right now, and then I'll take the element out of it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're working with the fuel cask.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: That's a bad place to put it (the HTC?), Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's a bad place to put it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, I know what I have to do, Al.
And I'm standing here not doing it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, maybe I need to move the SIDE on.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You can log me for my first
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's real interesting, as we put out this ALSEP right...(Hearing Gibson) There's one thing that's pretty obvious, as we're setting out the components of the ALSEP here.
(And that) is: I just hope that these thermal coatings don't have to stay as white as they are right now, because there's just no way (of keeping them absolutely clean).
With all this dust, there's just no possibility...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...of not getting them a little bit dirty.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Little bit dirty isn't the word for it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This is going to be a real problem, I guess, if thermally they've got to maintain that (clean) coating.
Because there's just no way you can do it.
Everything that touches the ground picks it up.
Your suit is about half dirty because the (LEC) strap landed on it.
It's a little dirty even on top of the fuel element, there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait just a minute now before you get turned away.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I guess we'll just have to make allowances for things like that when we build them.
They sure are going to get dirty.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (I'll) put the tool and the dome aside.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll get out the cask removal tool.
(Pause) Why don't you stand over there, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I've got to go back to
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah; it's not...(Pause) There you go.
(Long Pause) You got to be kidding.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Make sure it (the removal tool)'s screwed all the way down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That could make a guy mad, you know it?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me undo it a minute, and try it a different way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It can really get you mad.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, Al put the tool on, screwed it all the way down, and the fuel element would not come out of the cask.
He's taking the tool off, and he's working her again.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It really kind of surprises me.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I tell you what worries me, Pete.
If I pull on it too hard, it's a very delicate lock mechanism.
Maybe (I should) not push the pins in quite so far, and wiggle it a little.
I just get the feeling that it's hot and swelled in there or something.
I can sure feel the heat, though, on my hands.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We've really got a problem, I guess.
You know, it's got a three-pin removal tool, so I tried using different pins in different holes.
That doesn't appear to have any effect.
You know, everything operates just exactly like it does in the training mock-ups and up at GE (General Electric Corporation).
The only problem is, it just won't come out of the cask.
I am suspicious that it's just swollen in there or something and friction's holding it in.
But it's such a delicate tool, I really hate to pull on it too hard.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think what we can do...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...go get that hammer and bang on the side of it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But I want to try and put the back end in under that lip there and pry her out.
Let me go get the hammer.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me get the tool off; it's starting to warm up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, when you're working on that, try to make sure you've got the pins all the way in.
Tighten up on it; then you can try pushing down on it a little, before you pull it out.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Pete) Don't touch these needles (the pins on the removal tool); if these break off, that's all she wrote.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Rotate and try it this way.
(Pause) (The removal tool) drives in there just like it's going to do the job.
Only it doesn't do the job.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh man, look at this dust fly.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Let me) get those pins in there again.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're not getting those pins all the way in.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They're not in now because I'm lining them up.
Now they are all the way in.
That bottom one down there's...(Pause) Now, my recommendation would be pound on the casket, then...you know.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Give it a few more pounds.
(Pause) Got to beat harder than that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughs, cheering him on) Come on, Conrad!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It fits in the RTG real well!
It's just the cask was holding it in on the side.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Don't come to the Moon without a hammer.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's make our move (and carry the ALSEP out to the deployment site).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: The fuel element is in the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait a minute; don't move...No, wait a minute, Al.
Have you got a strap around your boot?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, this thermal coating doesn't mean a thing here (because it gets dirty so quickly).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That thing is really getting covered with dirt.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I hope they made allowances for it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It doesn't look like it (is properly attached).
Let me look at that just a second.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, feel the heat off that machine.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (In a conspiratorial tone) Almost as hot as the Sun!
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay; let me go scout...smoke over the area.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's go right off to our little mound over there; how does that grab you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Long Pause, probably while they make sure the packages are secure on the carrybar)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, it's kinda...The thing doesn't...Well, let's try it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You've got the UHT, tongs, and (SIDE) subpallet.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I can tell this is going to be a workload.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Houston) How long did you say our shadow was...(that is), the LM's shadow, 150 feet?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Take your time, Al; I'll just go on out...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, I'm just going out to scout the area, that's all.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'm going to set it down and rest...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You know, they ought to build this equipment for lunar operations some other color besides white.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Laughs; Pause) I'm going to go right up to the Head Crater, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, if you're going to do anything...(Pause) Okay; let me move another couple of...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got any direction you want me to go?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, it looks to me like either the direction you're headed is good, or the one a little bit more to the right.
You're going to have to go far enough so we don't end up in one of the craters when we furthest south deploy.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But I want to go 10 degrees off our takeoff angle; and I think I'm headed out about that way now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad
